domain:
In an automated facility, a fleet of robots is deployed, where each one is recognized by a unique identifier. These robots perform tasks based on their function, some are cleaning robots, while others serve as transport robots. In the facility, the robots are assigned to a set of tasks, each distinguished by a unique code. Throughout the operation, a robot may be assigned to perform various tasks. 

When a robot performs a task, the execution  is identified by a unique code and occurs at a specific starting time. Finally, each robot is assigned to a location within the facility, which can be either a corridor or a room.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Robot in the relationship with TaskExecution?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a task execution can exist without any associated robot.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a task execution must have at least one associated robot.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, TaskExecution can exist without Robot.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,TaskExecution must have at least one Robot.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,TaskExecution must have at least one Robot.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of TaskExecution in the relationship with Robot?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a robot can exist without any associated task execution.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a robot must have at least one associated task execution.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Robot can exist without TaskExecution.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Robot must have at least one TaskExecution.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Robot can exist without TaskExecution.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of Task in the relationship with TaskExecution?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a task execution can exist without any associated task.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a task execution must have at least one associated task.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, TaskExecution can exist without Task.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,TaskExecution must have at least one Task.
questions.selected: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,TaskExecution must have at least one Task.

questions.question:
What is the minimum cardinality of TaskExecution in the relationship with Task?

For example:
1. Minimum cardinality of zero, a task can exist without any associated task execution.
2. Minimum cardinality of one, a task must have at least one associated task execution.

Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Task can exist without TaskExecution.
questions.option_2: Option 2: One as minimum cardinality,Task must have at least one TaskExecution.
questions.selected: Option 1: Zero as minimum cardinality, Task can exist without TaskExecution.

questions.question:
Should TaskExecution be modeled as an association class linking Robot and Task, or as a regular class with two separate associations?

For example:
1. Association class: TaskExecution exists only as part of the relationship between Robot and Task. A task execution cannot exist without the link between Robot and Task.
2. Regular class: TaskExecution is a class that has links to Robot and Task. In this case multiple task executions can exist for every Robot and Task.


Which option is better for you?
questions.option_1: Option 1: Association class, TaskExecution as association class linking Robot and Task.
questions.option_2: Option 2: Regular class, TaskExecution as separate class with two associations.
questions.selected: Option 2: Regular class, TaskExecution as separate class with two associations.
